metadata.dataset_description: Monthly totals of international airline passengers (passengers) from Jan 1949 to Dec 1960. Classic univariate time series with clear upward trend and yearly seasonality (multiplicative).
known_data_quality_issues: Datetime column stored as string (format 'YYYY-MM') — parse to datetime and set as index
Column name contains special character '#' which can cause issues in some processing pipelines
Non-stationary series with strong upward trend (detected by linear regression r≈0.924)
Clear annual seasonality (peaks in Jul/Aug) and multiplicative behavior (variance grows with level)
Moderate right skew and heteroscedasticity (variance increases over time)
Small sample size (144 monthly observations) — limits very large ML models without augmentation/feature engineering

preprocessing_steps.reason: Multiplicative seasonality and increasing variance (heteroscedasticity); log stabilizes variance and converts multiplicative effects to additive.
Strong upward linear trend detected (non-stationary). Remove linear trend to help stationarity and make seasonality clearer.
First-order differencing to remove remaining non-stationarity after detrending and to stabilize mean for many forecasting models.
Reduce short-term noise while preserving seasonal pattern for feature extraction or ML model training (use small window like 3).
Standardize scale for machine-learning models (e.g., LSTM, RandomForest with scalers) so features are comparable; applied after transformations.

metadata.licenses_or_usage_rights: Unknown — check original data source for license and redistribution terms (this file appears to be the classic AirPassengers dataset).
metadata.security_or_privacy_notes: No personal or sensitive information detected. Standard data protection practices apply.
